title: Urachne molinioides
taxonomy: Urachne molinioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is native to Africa. The classification of Urachne molinioides places it within the subfamily Aristidoideae. Further classification details include placing it in the tribe Aristideae and in the genus Urachne. The specific classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Poaceae family.

intro: Urachne molinioides, also known as the molinia grass, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Urachne molinioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
